wide-delete-insert-wipe.scope: any delete+insert reprocess whose delete-set spans many slices/streams; policy applies repo-wide
wide-delete-insert-wipe.symptom: dbt reports failure (OOM), but minutes later the target window is empty — months of data gone with no error attributing the wipe
wide-delete-insert-wipe.title: Wide delete+insert OOMs, then the background delete silently wipes the window

hazards.title: argMax "latest/quarter-end" marts silently serve the last ingested day when a source halts

rules.text: Never a wide delete+insert — the background delete can complete after dbt reports failure and silently wipe the window; reprocess per slice/partition.
insert_overwrite REPLACEs whole partitions: partition grain must equal the overwrite grain; never combine with unique_key, slice vars, or staged batches.
Follow the required workflow in AGENTS.md; check docs/lessons/INDEX.md before diagnosing a data-quality symptom.
The api:/granularity:/tier tag contract and typed column schema are CI-enforced. Renaming/retiring requires updating semantic/authoring and grepping the metrics-dashboard repo's SQL by FROM/JOIN.

hazards.title: Global-frontier carry-forward silently drops thin series

rules.text: Never a wide delete+insert — the background delete can complete after dbt reports failure and silently wipe the window; reprocess per slice/partition.
insert_overwrite REPLACEs whole partitions: partition grain must equal the overwrite grain; never combine with unique_key, slice vars, or staged batches.
Follow the required workflow in AGENTS.md; check docs/lessons/INDEX.md before diagnosing a data-quality symptom.
Anchor carry-forward at the per-entity frontier, never a global max(date) — thin pools fall off a shared frontier and accrete permanent gaps.

rules.text: Never a wide delete+insert — the background delete can complete after dbt reports failure and silently wipe the window; reprocess per slice/partition.
insert_overwrite REPLACEs whole partitions: partition grain must equal the overwrite grain; never combine with unique_key, slice vars, or staged batches.
Follow the required workflow in AGENTS.md; check docs/lessons/INDEX.md before diagnosing a data-quality symptom.
Anchor carry-forward at the per-entity frontier, never a global max(date) — thin pools fall off a shared frontier and accrete permanent gaps.
The api:/granularity:/tier tag contract and typed column schema are CI-enforced. Renaming/retiring requires updating semantic/authoring and grepping the metrics-dashboard repo's SQL by FROM/JOIN.

rules.text: Never a wide delete+insert — the background delete can complete after dbt reports failure and silently wipe the window; reprocess per slice/partition.
insert_overwrite REPLACEs whole partitions: partition grain must equal the overwrite grain; never combine with unique_key, slice vars, or staged batches.
Follow the required workflow in AGENTS.md; check docs/lessons/INDEX.md before diagnosing a data-quality symptom.
Scoped start_month append only into EMPTY months (populated months double); reprocess with reprocess_overwrite one slice at a time.
The api:/granularity:/tier tag contract and typed column schema are CI-enforced. Renaming/retiring requires updating semantic/authoring and grepping the metrics-dashboard repo's SQL by FROM/JOIN.

contract.ground_truth: on-chain eth_getLogs over the contract + block range

hazards.title: Append decode watermark drops late-arriving logs forever
Decode watermarks must be embedded literals — scalar subqueries can't prune partitions
An incremental model created before its input existed stays empty forever
Code 241 with a "(total)" server-wide limit means your query is the victim, not the culprit
Staged/batched refreshes against insert_overwrite models wipe months via REPLACE PARTITION
Backfill order matters — cumulative downstreams need history first
Stage start_date later than real first activity silently truncates history

rules.text: Never a wide delete+insert — the background delete can complete after dbt reports failure and silently wipe the window; reprocess per slice/partition.
insert_overwrite REPLACEs whole partitions: partition grain must equal the overwrite grain; never combine with unique_key, slice vars, or staged batches.
Follow the required workflow in AGENTS.md; check docs/lessons/INDEX.md before diagnosing a data-quality symptom.
No lookback: raw logs backfilled below the append watermark are dropped forever by normal runs; recover known gap months with gap_window_refresh.py, never the daily runner.
Watermarks are render-time literals (partition pruning); never revert to a scalar-subquery bound.
Seed a new decode model once with dbt run --full-refresh; an empty target has no watermark and full-scans the raw source every run. New decode models crash dbt docs generate until first built.
After first build, verify the table is non-empty: a green daily run on an empty incremental stays empty forever (bootstrap can't reach history).

validation: raw-vs-decoded monthly parity for the contract (see tests/data_quality/)

hazards.title: Append decode watermark drops late-arriving logs forever
Decode watermarks must be embedded literals — scalar subqueries can't prune partitions
An incremental model created before its input existed stays empty forever
Code 241 with a "(total)" server-wide limit means your query is the victim, not the culprit
Staged/batched refreshes against insert_overwrite models wipe months via REPLACE PARTITION
Backfill order matters — cumulative downstreams need history first
Stage start_date later than real first activity silently truncates history

rules.text: Never a wide delete+insert — the background delete can complete after dbt reports failure and silently wipe the window; reprocess per slice/partition.
insert_overwrite REPLACEs whole partitions: partition grain must equal the overwrite grain; never combine with unique_key, slice vars, or staged batches.
Follow the required workflow in AGENTS.md; check docs/lessons/INDEX.md before diagnosing a data-quality symptom.
No lookback: raw logs backfilled below the append watermark are dropped forever by normal runs; recover known gap months with gap_window_refresh.py, never the daily runner.
Watermarks are render-time literals (partition pruning); never revert to a scalar-subquery bound.
Seed a new decode model once with dbt run --full-refresh; an empty target has no watermark and full-scans the raw source every run. New decode models crash dbt docs generate until first built.
After first build, verify the table is non-empty: a green daily run on an empty incremental stays empty forever (bootstrap can't reach history).

validation: raw-vs-decoded monthly parity for the contract (see tests/data_quality/)

An incremental model created before its input existed stays empty forever
Code 241 with a "(total)" server-wide limit means your query is the victim, not the culprit
Staged/batched refreshes against insert_overwrite models wipe months via REPLACE PARTITION
Backfill order matters — cumulative downstreams need history first
Stage start_date later than real first activity silently truncates history

rules.text: Never a wide delete+insert — the background delete can complete after dbt reports failure and silently wipe the window; reprocess per slice/partition.
insert_overwrite REPLACEs whole partitions: partition grain must equal the overwrite grain; never combine with unique_key, slice vars, or staged batches.
Follow the required workflow in AGENTS.md; check docs/lessons/INDEX.md before diagnosing a data-quality symptom.
No lookback: raw logs backfilled below the append watermark are dropped forever by normal runs; recover known gap months with gap_window_refresh.py, never the daily runner.
Watermarks are render-time literals (partition pruning); never revert to a scalar-subquery bound.
Seed a new decode model once with dbt run --full-refresh; an empty target has no watermark and full-scans the raw source every run. New decode models crash dbt docs generate until first built.
After first build, verify the table is non-empty: a green daily run on an empty incremental stays empty forever (bootstrap can't reach history).

validation: raw-vs-decoded monthly parity for the contract (see tests/data_quality/)

contract.ground_truth: on-chain eth_getLogs over the contract + block range

Code 241 with a "(total)" server-wide limit means your query is the victim, not the culprit
Staged/batched refreshes against insert_overwrite models wipe months via REPLACE PARTITION

Backfill order matters — cumulative downstreams need history first
Stage start_date later than real first activity silently truncates history

hazards.title: An incremental model created before its input existed stays empty forever
Code 241 with a "(total)" server-wide limit means your query is the victim, not the culprit
Backfill order matters — cumulative downstreams need history first
Duplicate seed rows in an append chain carry a constant error forward every day
A cumulative chain that builds the frontier day before its inputs settle freezes the hole

rules.text: Never a wide delete+insert — the background delete can complete after dbt reports failure and silently wipe the window; reprocess per slice/partition.
insert_overwrite REPLACEs whole partitions: partition grain must equal the overwrite grain; never combine with unique_key, slice vars, or staged batches.
Follow the required workflow in AGENTS.md; check docs/lessons/INDEX.md before diagnosing a data-quality symptom.
After first build, verify the table is non-empty: a green daily run on an empty incremental stays empty forever (bootstrap can't reach history).
Cumulative: backfill history FIRST, chronologically. Forward runs never repair a wrong initial condition; a duplicate seed row propagates forever.

A frontier day built before its inputs settled stays frozen forever (the incremental branch never revisits it) even after the upstream self-heals — repair with the model's reprocess window, not the daily runner.

hazards.title: An incremental model created before its input existed stays empty forever
Code 241 with a "(total)" server-wide limit means your query is the victim, not the culprit
Staged/batched refreshes against insert_overwrite models wipe months via REPLACE PARTITION
>100 partitions per INSERT fails (252) and Cloud blocks raising the cap (452)
Scoped append over already-populated months exactly doubles the data
Backfill order matters — cumulative downstreams need history first
Stage start_date later than real first activity silently truncates history
Duplicate seed rows in an append chain carry a constant error forward every day
A cumulative chain that builds the frontier day before its inputs settle freezes the hole

rules.text: Never a wide delete+insert — the background delete can complete after dbt reports failure and silently wipe the window; reprocess per slice/partition.
insert_overwrite REPLACEs whole partitions: partition grain must equal the overwrite grain; never combine with unique_key, slice vars, or staged batches.
Follow the required workflow in AGENTS.md; check docs/lessons/INDEX.md before diagnosing a data-quality symptom.
After first build, verify the table is non-empty: a green daily run on an empty incremental stays empty forever (bootstrap can't reach history).
The strategy branches on run vars: scoped windows must take the append path and append only into EMPTY months; verify which branch a given invocation resolves to before running.
Cumulative: backfill history FIRST, chronologically. Forward runs never repair a wrong initial condition; a duplicate seed row propagates forever.

A frontier day built before its inputs settled stays frozen forever (the incremental branch never revisits it) even after the upstream self-heals — repair with the model's reprocess window, not the daily runner.

hazards.title: An incremental model created before its input existed stays empty forever
Code 241 with a "(total)" server-wide limit means your query is the victim, not the culprit
Staged/batched refreshes against insert_overwrite models wipe months via REPLACE PARTITION
>100 partitions per INSERT fails (252) and Cloud blocks raising the cap (452)
Backfill order matters — cumulative downstreams need history first
Stage start_date later than real first activity silently truncates history

Follow the required workflow in AGENTS.md; check docs/lessons/INDEX.md before diagnosing a data-quality symptom.
After first build, verify the table is non-empty: a green daily run on an empty incremental stays empty forever (bootstrap can't reach history).

hazards.title: An incremental model created before its input existed stays empty forever
Code 241 with a "(total)" server-wide limit means your query is the victim, not the culprit
Staged/batched refreshes against insert_overwrite models wipe months via REPLACE PARTITION
Scoped append over already-populated months exactly doubles the data
Backfill order matters — cumulative downstreams need history first
Stage start_date later than real first activity silently truncates history
Duplicate seed rows in an append chain carry a constant error forward every day
A cumulative chain that builds the frontier day before its inputs settle freezes the hole

rules.text: Never a wide delete+insert — the background delete can complete after dbt reports failure and silently wipe the window; reprocess per slice/partition.
insert_overwrite REPLACEs whole partitions: partition grain must equal the overwrite grain; never combine with unique_key, slice vars, or staged batches.
Follow the required workflow in AGENTS.md; check docs/lessons/INDEX.md before diagnosing a data-quality symptom.
After first build, verify the table is non-empty: a green daily run on an empty incremental stays empty forever (bootstrap can't reach history).
The strategy branches on run vars: scoped windows must take the append path and append only into EMPTY months; verify which branch a given invocation resolves to before running.
Cumulative: backfill history FIRST, chronologically. Forward runs never repair a wrong initial condition; a duplicate seed row propagates forever.

A frontier day built before its inputs settled stays frozen forever (the incremental branch never revisits it) even after the upstream self-heals — repair with the model's reprocess window, not the daily runner.

hazards.title: Global-frontier carry-forward silently drops thin series

rules.text: Never a wide delete+insert — the background delete can complete after dbt reports failure and silently wipe the window; reprocess per slice/partition.
insert_overwrite REPLACEs whole partitions: partition grain must equal the overwrite grain; never combine with unique_key, slice vars, or staged batches.

Anchor carry-forward at the per-entity frontier, never a global max(date) — thin pools fall off a shared frontier and accrete permanent gaps.

Follow the required workflow in AGENTS.md; check docs/lessons/INDEX.md before diagnosing a data-quality symptom.
After first build, verify the table is non-empty: a green daily run on an empty incremental stays empty forever (bootstrap can't reach history).

Staged/batched refreshes against insert_overwrite models wipe months via REPLACE PARTITION
Scoped append over already-populated months exactly doubles the data
Backfill order matters — cumulative downstreams need history first
Stage start_date later than real first activity silently truncates history

Follow the required workflow in AGENTS.md; check docs/lessons/INDEX.md before diagnosing a data-quality symptom.
After first build, verify the table is non-empty: a green daily run on an empty incremental stays empty forever (bootstrap can't reach history).

hazards.title: An incremental model created before its input existed stays empty forever
Code 241 with a "(total)" server-wide limit means your query is the victim, not the culprit
Staged/batched refreshes against insert_overwrite models wipe months via REPLACE PARTITION
Scoped append over already-populated months exactly doubles the data
Backfill order matters — cumulative downstreams need history first
Stage start_date later than real first activity silently truncates history
Duplicate seed rows in an append chain carry a constant error forward every day
A cumulative chain that builds the frontier day before its inputs settle freezes the hole

rules.text: Never a wide delete+insert — the background delete can complete after dbt reports failure and silently wipe the window; reprocess per slice/partition.
insert_overwrite REPLACEs whole partitions: partition grain must equal the overwrite grain; never combine with unique_key, slice vars, or staged batches.
Follow the required workflow in AGENTS.md; check docs/lessons/INDEX.md before diagnosing a data-quality symptom.
After first build, verify the table is non-empty: a green daily run on an empty incremental stays empty forever (bootstrap can't reach history).
The strategy branches on run vars: scoped windows must take the append path and append only into EMPTY months; verify which branch a given invocation resolves to before running.
Cumulative: backfill history FIRST, chronologically. Forward runs never repair a wrong initial condition; a duplicate seed row propagates forever.

A frontier day built before its inputs settled stays frozen forever (the incremental branch never revisits it) even after the upstream self-heals — repair with the model's reprocess window, not the daily runner.

invariants.text: Exactly one row per (date, protocol, reserve_address, user_address). Duplicate seed-day rows under ReplacingMergeTree (read without FINAL) used to inject the prior scaled_balance twice into the carry-forward and permanently double every holder's balance (exact 2x spikes on 2026-06-19 and 2026-06-21 across all reserves).

Incremental carry-forward must add prev_scaled as a constant (sum(diffs)+prev), never UNION ALL it into the event stream as a synthetic delta — the latter double-counts when the seed day has unmerged duplicate parts.
A holder with zero diffs on day D must keep the prior day's scaled_balance (index accrual only in underlying). A ~2.0 day-over-day ratio on idle holders is a seed bug, not real supply.

contract.reprocess_runbook: corrupted/frozen month: dbt run with vars {start_month, end_month, reprocess_overwrite: true} — seeds from last good day BEFORE the window (delete+insert). Full history: staged scripts/full_refresh/refresh.py per reserve_symbol. Always rebuild cohorts AFTER balances, in a separate invocation (never same append-mode run as the source).

compare_aave_scaled_balances.py vs RPC on spot dates

A cumulative chain that builds the frontier day before its inputs settle freezes the hole

invariants.text: reserve_amount == token_amount == the physical pool token balance (cumulative sum of signed event deltas; swap fees are NOT subtracted, Flash contributes only the retained fee, Burn/removal-of-principal is clamped). A negative reserve, or reserve_amount != token_amount, means swap-fee-subtraction drift was reintroduced.

Every (pool, token) pair should have a row for every calendar day from its first event; a thin pool that skips a day can fall off the single global max(date) carry-forward frontier (global-frontier-carry-forward) and accrete gaps.

contract.reprocess_runbook: dbt run --full-refresh -s int_execution_pools_swapr_v3_daily (non-incremental branch rebuilds the per-pool dense calendar from inception, no carry-forward seed), then rebuild the pools balances/TVL downstream tables

rules.text: Never a wide delete+insert — the background delete can complete after dbt reports failure and silently wipe the window; reprocess per slice/partition.
insert_overwrite REPLACEs whole partitions: partition grain must equal the overwrite grain; never combine with unique_key, slice vars, or staged batches.
Follow the required workflow in AGENTS.md; check docs/lessons/INDEX.md before diagnosing a data-quality symptom.
After first build, verify the table is non-empty: a green daily run on an empty incremental stays empty forever (bootstrap can't reach history).
The strategy branches on run vars: scoped windows must take the append path and append only into EMPTY months; verify which branch a given invocation resolves to before running.
Cumulative: backfill history FIRST, chronologically. Forward runs never repair a wrong initial condition; a duplicate seed row propagates forever.

A frontier day built before its inputs settled stays frozen forever (the incremental branch never revisits it) even after the upstream self-heals — repair with the model's reprocess window, not the daily runner.

invariants.text: Exactly one row per (week, stream, symbol, user). Downstream cohort marts read WITHOUT FINAL and aggregate with sum()/countIf(), so any duplicate copy doubles fees and users one-for-one.
annual_rolling_fees needs the full 52 weeks of history behind every rewritten week; never write a week without its window_history_weeks feed.

contract.reprocess_runbook: reprocess_overwrite=true ONE slice at a time (vars slice=stream:SYMBOL); never whole-window (OOM + background-delete wipe), never scoped append into populated months (exact 2x)

rules.text: Never a wide delete+insert — the background delete can complete after dbt reports failure and silently wipe the window; reprocess per slice/partition.
insert_overwrite REPLACEs whole partitions: partition grain must equal the overwrite grain; never combine with unique_key, slice vars, or staged batches.

After first build, verify the table is non-empty: a green daily run on an empty incremental stays empty forever (bootstrap can't reach history).
The strategy branches on run vars: scoped windows must take the append path and append only into EMPTY months; verify which branch a given invocation resolves to before running.
Cumulative: backfill history FIRST, chronologically. Forward runs never repair a wrong initial condition; a duplicate seed row propagates forever.

A frontier day built before its inputs settled stays frozen forever (the incremental branch never revisits it) even after the upstream self-heals — repair with the model's reprocess window, not the daily runner.
Scoped start_month append only into EMPTY months (populated months double); reprocess with reprocess_overwrite one slice at a time.

validation: duplicate-excess by month and stream: count() - uniqExact(week, stream_type, symbol, user) must be 0

rules.text: Never a wide delete+insert — the background delete can complete after dbt reports failure and silently wipe the window; reprocess per slice/partition.
insert_overwrite REPLACEs whole partitions: partition grain must equal the overwrite grain; never combine with unique_key, slice vars, or staged batches.
Follow the required workflow in AGENTS.md; check docs/lessons/INDEX.md before diagnosing a data-quality symptom.
After first build, verify the table is non-empty: a green daily run on an empty incremental stays empty forever (bootstrap can't reach history).
The strategy branches on run vars: scoped windows must take the append path and append only into EMPTY months; verify which branch a given invocation resolves to before running.
Cumulative: backfill history FIRST, chronologically. Forward runs never repair a wrong initial condition; a duplicate seed row propagates forever.

A frontier day built before its inputs settled stays frozen forever (the incremental branch never revisits it) even after the upstream self-heals — repair with the model's reprocess window, not the daily runner.

insert_overwrite REPLACEs whole partitions: partition grain must equal the overwrite grain; never combine with unique_key, slice vars, or staged batches.
Follow the required workflow in AGENTS.md; check docs/lessons/INDEX.md before diagnosing a data-quality symptom.
Anchor carry-forward at the per-entity frontier, never a global max(date) — thin pools fall off a shared frontier and accrete permanent gaps.
